meta.description: The Crude Oracle company intelligence notes. SAMPLE content — anonymised placeholder profiles. Not recommendations. See docs/DAILY_UPDATE_GUIDE.md.

companies.description: Large-cap integrated energy company with global upstream, LNG and marketing operations. Placeholder profile — replace with real coverage.
companies.production_exposure: ~2.3 mboe/d (placeholder), roughly 60% liquids / 40% gas, diversified across OECD and non-OECD basins.
companies.reserves_note: Reserve life around 9 years on a proved basis (placeholder); resource depth supplemented by LNG contract portfolio.
companies.financial_health_note: Investment-grade balance sheet, gearing within stated target band, dividend covered at conservative planning prices.
companies.valuation_note: Trades at a discount to US peers on cash-flow multiples (placeholder observation) — the 'UK listing discount' debate is a live theme.
companies.management_note: Capital allocation framework prioritises base dividend, then balance-sheet strength, then buybacks. Track record of hitting guidance.
companies.catalyst: Capital markets day; quarterly shareholder-return updates; portfolio rotation announcements.
companies.risk: Oil price downside, windfall/political tax risk, long-run demand uncertainty, execution on transition projects.
companies.latest_update: Maintained buyback run-rate at Q2 results; reiterated full-year production guidance. (Sample note.)

companies.description: Permian-focused independent E&P with a decade-plus of core inventory at sub-$45 WTI break-evens (placeholder profile).
companies.production_exposure: ~700 kboe/d (placeholder), oil-weighted, concentrated in the Midland and Delaware basins.
companies.reserves_note: Inventory depth is the key debate: management guides to 12+ years of core locations (placeholder).
companies.financial_health_note: Net debt / EBITDA below 1.0x; base-plus-variable dividend framework returns the majority of free cash flow.
companies.valuation_note: Valuation tracks WTI strip and inventory-per-share metrics; consolidation has re-rated the basin (placeholder observation).
companies.management_note: Founder-led, strong operational track record, disciplined M&A history.
companies.catalyst: Well productivity trends, bolt-on acquisitions, variable dividend declarations.
companies.risk: WTI weakness, service cost inflation, degradation of well productivity, basin takeaway constraints.
companies.latest_update: Raised full-year oil guidance ~2% on strong well performance; service costs flat sequentially. (Sample note.)

companies.description: Mid-cap UKCS producer with operated hub infrastructure and a mix of producing assets and near-field development options (placeholder profile).
companies.production_exposure: ~45 kboe/d (placeholder), Brent-linked liquids with associated NBP gas.
companies.reserves_note: 2P reserves ~150 mmboe (placeholder); near-field tie-back inventory dependent on fiscal stability.
companies.financial_health_note: RBL headroom adequate; hedging covers ~60% of near-term production; decommissioning liabilities are the long-term balance-sheet consideration.
companies.valuation_note: Trades at a steep discount to NAV (placeholder observation) reflecting UK fiscal risk — the central controversy for the whole UKCS peer group.
companies.management_note: Experienced North Sea operating team; capital allocation paused pending fiscal clarity.
companies.catalyst: UK fiscal regime decisions, reserves updates, consolidation among UKCS independents.
companies.risk: Windfall tax extension/expansion, licensing policy shifts, single-basin concentration, decommissioning cost inflation.
companies.latest_update: Deferred FID on a tie-back project citing fiscal uncertainty; production guidance unchanged. (Sample note.)

companies.description: Large-scale LNG producer and exporter with contracted liquefaction capacity and brownfield expansion optionality (placeholder profile).
companies.production_exposure: ~45 mtpa liquefaction capacity (placeholder), predominantly sold under long-term SPAs with oil- and Henry Hub-linked pricing.
companies.reserves_note: Feedgas sourced from US market — resource risk is commercial rather than geological.
companies.financial_health_note: Project-level debt amortising on schedule; corporate leverage declining; buyback authorised.
companies.valuation_note: Valued on contracted cash-flow duration plus expansion option value (placeholder observation).
companies.management_note: Commercial team regarded as best-in-class at SPA structuring; disciplined FID criteria.
companies.catalyst: Expansion FIDs, new long-term SPAs, US permitting policy developments.
companies.risk: Global LNG oversupply into the late 2020s, construction cost inflation, US permitting/political risk, contract counterparty risk.
companies.latest_update: Signed a new 20-year SPA with an Asian buyer (sample note); expansion FID targeted within 12 months.
